---
title: "Cross-subject concat with contracts"
subtitle: "Stack subjects with frame validation at the API surface."
jupyter: python3
sidebar: cookbook
---

## Goal

Concatenate per-subject 4-D BOLD runs into one series for a group
reduction, with the shared spatial frame validated *before* the stack
happens — not discovered after a wrong-frame subject has polluted the
mean.

## Code

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd().parents[1] / "src"))

import numpy as np
import neuroim as ni

repo = Path.cwd().parents[1] if Path.cwd().name == "cookbook" else Path.cwd()

# Two "subjects" in the same frame (the fixture stands in for two runs).
subj_a = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_bold.nii.gz"))
subj_b = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_bold.nii.gz"))
mask = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_mask.nii.gz"), type="vol")

merged = subj_a.concat(subj_b)               # also: ni.concat(subj_a, subj_b)
roi = ni.ROICoords(np.argwhere(np.asarray(mask.data)), space=mask.space)
group_mean_ts = merged.series_roi(roi).values.mean(axis=1)

merged.shape, group_mean_ts.shape
```

`concat` is available as the instance method `a.concat(*rest)` and the
module function `ni.concat(*vecs)`.

## What's guaranteed

- Every pair's spatial frame is checked before concatenation. A
  subject in a different frame never reaches the stack.
- `series_roi` then re-validates the mask frame against the merged
  series — the contract holds end to end.

## What survives the file

`merged.series_roi(roi)` returns an `ROIExtractionResult` with a
Receipt; the `input_space_hash` reflects the subjects' shared frame.
If you build a group map and `to_nibabel()` it, that hash rides the
file.

## Trapdoors

```{python}
#| error: true
from neuroim.neuro_space import NeuroSpace

wrong_frame = ni.DenseNeuroVec(
    np.asarray(subj_b.data),
    NeuroSpace(tuple(subj_b.space.dim), spacing=(9, 9, 9, 1)),
)
subj_a.concat(wrong_frame)
```

A frame-mismatched subject raises `ValueError`:
`spatial contract mismatch: NeuroVecs must have same spatial
dimensions and affine; …`. The failure is at the API surface, before
any array is stacked.

## See also

- [Concept: NeuroSpace](../concepts/space.qmd) — `compatible_with`, the leaf check.
- [Concept: Contract failures](../concepts/contract-failures.qmd) — the `CONCAT-SPACE` refusal.
- [Evidence](../evidence/index.qmd) — runnable checks for multi-subject spatial validation.
